parents.subject: LU-12187 lod: calculate parity stripe count from data stripes

commit.subject: LU-0000 lod: initialize EC files with RDONLY FLR state
commit.message:
LU-0000 lod: initialize EC files with RDONLY FLR state

Erasure coded (EC) files should follow the same FLR
(File Level Redundancy) lifecycle as mirrored files.
Parity components in EC files are conceptually
identical to mirror copies - they're redundant data
that can become stale and need resynchronization.

This patch implements Phase 1: Initialize EC files
with LCM_FL_RDONLY state instead of LCM_FL_NONE, so
they enter the FLR state machine from the beginning.

Changes:
- Add ldo_ec_count field to track parity component
  count
- Add lod_comp_parity() helper to identify parity
  components
- Set EC files to RDONLY state during file creation
  in lod_qos_parse_config()
- Set EC files to RDONLY state when loading layout
  from disk in lod_striping_reload()
- Write FLR state to disk for EC files in lod_lov.c
- Allow EC files to have FLR state without multiple
  mirrors in client-side lov_init_composite()
- Keep EC files in RDONLY state after resync
  completion

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I0bab733d1c43e2bf939546f8c3b7b9cd5b6aaf53
